rulings.case_name: V.O.S. Selections, Inc. v. United States
rulings.summary: Three-judge CIT panel ruled that IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs. The court held that 'regulate...importation' does not include tariff authority and that such an interpretation would violate the nondelegation doctrine.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether IEEPA's grant of authority to 'regulate...importation' authorizes the President to impose tariffs

timeline_events.event: CIT rules IEEPA does not authorize tariffs (Slip Op. 25-66)

rulings.court: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit
rulings.summary: Federal Circuit affirmed CIT ruling that IEEPA does not authorize tariffs. En banc court noted the tariffs were 'unbounded in scope, amount, and duration' and lacked any statutory basis.
rulings.source_name: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether the CIT correctly held that IEEPA does not grant tariff authority to the President

timeline_events.event: En banc opinion affirms: IEEPA does not authorize tariffs

rulings.case_name: Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump (consolidated with Trump v. V.O.S. Selections)
rulings.court: Supreme Court of the United States
rulings.summary: Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that IEEPA does not authorize the President to impose tariffs. Chief Justice Roberts wrote that 'when Congress has delegated its tariff powers, it has done so in explicit terms.' Approximately $129 billion in IEEPA duty deposits had been collected; ~19.2 million entries remained unliquidated.
rulings.defendant: Donald J. Trump, President of the United States
rulings.source_name: Supreme Court of the United States
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether IEEPA authorizes the President to impose tariffs on imports

timeline_events.event: Decision: 6-3, IEEPA does not authorize tariffs

rulings.case_name: Axle of Dearborn, Inc. v. Department of Commerce
rulings.summary: Challenges the use of IEEPA to eliminate the $800 de minimis duty-free threshold under 19 U.S.C. 1321. After the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariff authority, this case questions whether the ruling also forecloses IEEPA-based suspension of de minimis treatment.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether IEEPA authorizes the President to override the de minimis exemption Congress established in Section 1321

timeline_events.event: Complaint filed challenging de minimis suspension
Motion to lift stay (pending V.O.S. resolution)

rulings.case_name: Administrative Order 25-02: Stay of New IEEPA Cases
rulings.summary: Chief Judge Barnett established procedures for the flood of new IEEPA tariff cases. All new IEEPA tariff cases are automatically stayed upon commencement, pending a final unappealable decision in V.O.S. Selections.
rulings.key_legal_question: Administrative procedure for managing volume of IEEPA tariff litigation

timeline_events.event: Administrative order issued: auto-stay all new IEEPA cases

rulings.case_name: State of California et al. v. United States
rulings.summary: Two dozen states challenge the Section 122 temporary 10% import surcharge imposed after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs. Plaintiffs argue trade deficits are not 'balance-of-payments deficits' as required by the statute, and the surcharge exceeds Section 122's 15% cap and 150-day limit.
rulings.plaintiff: State of California et al. (24 states)
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether 'balance-of-payments deficits' in Section 122 includes trade deficits, and whether statutory caps are being respected

timeline_events.event: Supreme Court strikes down IEEPA tariffs
24 states file lawsuit challenging Section 122 surcharge

rulings.case_name: HMTX Industries, LLC v. United States
rulings.court: U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit
rulings.summary: Challenges the procedural basis for Section 301 tariff increases on Chinese goods. If the APA applies, future tariff modifications (including maritime vessel fees) would require notice-and-comment rulemaking.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether the Administrative Procedure Act applies to Section 301 tariff actions

timeline_events.event: USTR acknowledges ongoing litigation in Section 301 maritime action

rulings.summary: Joint resolution to terminate the national emergency declared under IEEPA for Canada tariffs (EO 14193). Passed the Senate on April 2, 2025 with bipartisan support. House blocked expedited consideration under a March 2025 rule change.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether Congress can terminate the IEEPA national emergency underlying Canada tariffs via joint resolution

timeline_events.event: Senate passes resolution with bipartisan support

rulings.summary: Second Senate joint resolution to terminate the Canada IEEPA emergency. Passed Senate October 29, 2025 with bipartisan support from Senators Klobuchar, Kaine, Paul, Warner, and Schumer.
rulings.key_legal_question: Congressional override of IEEPA emergency declaration for Canada tariffs

rulings.summary: Joint resolution to terminate the national emergency underlying worldwide reciprocal tariffs (April 2, 2025). Introduced by Senators Wyden, Paul, Schumer, Kaine, Shaheen, Welch, and Warren. Referred to Finance Committee.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether Congress can terminate the IEEPA emergency underlying global reciprocal tariffs

rulings.summary: After the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs, the President immediately invoked Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 to impose a 10% ad valorem surcharge on most imports for 150 days (Feb. 24 - July 24, 2026). First use of Section 122 in over 50 years.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether Section 122 authorizes a 10% import surcharge based on trade deficit as a balance-of-payments problem

timeline_events.event: Supreme Court strikes down IEEPA tariffs

rulings.summary: Bill to repeal Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 entirely, removing the President's balance-of-payments tariff authority. Introduced in response to the Section 122 surcharge invoked after the Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs.
rulings.key_legal_question: Whether Congress will repeal the President's Section 122 balance-of-payments tariff authority
